title: eryngium raulinii
intro: Eryngium raulinii, also known as Raul's Sea Holly, is a flowering plant native to Mexico that belongs to the family Apiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Eryngium raulinii include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture for herbal use, and its flowers, leaves, and roots are often used in traditional remedies.
